facts.text: Manhasset Hills sits atop the Harbor Hill Moraine, a terminal moraine formed by Wisconsin glaciers and named after Harbor Hill, Nassau County’s highest point.
The Long Island Railroad reached Manhasset Hills in 1898, sparking a wave of wealthy New Yorkers building country homes with easy city access.
In 1956, Gerace & Catagna Inc. began developing the Americana Manhasset, a masonry‑based project that would shape the community’s modern look.
Manhasset Hills is known for its concentration of distinct local merchants and high‑end premium shops, a hallmark of its North Shore character.
The town’s Chamber of Commerce chronicles its history, preserving stories from early settlement to today’s thriving local economy.
